title: DEPLOYMENT DISTRIBUTED FROM A SINGLE MICRO-SOFTWARE
abstract: Various embodiments of the invention serve the purpose of efficiently deploying unique firmware for a plurality of processor circuitries. According to various examples, this is achieved by loading a first firmware and executing the first firmware and, based on said executing of the first firmware, generating a second firmware.
claims:
Claims Patent Claims 1. A method of operating a processor circuitry (101), comprising : - loading, from a non-volatile memory (102), a first firmware (110) and executing the first firmware (110), - based on said executing of the first firmware (110) : generating a second firmware (110) and writing the second firmware (110) to the non-volatile memory (102), and - in response to said generating of the second firmware (110) : disabling further repetitions of said executing of the first firmware (110) . 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the second firmware (110) is generated from the first firmware (110) or from a third firmware, wherein the second firmware (110) is uniquely identi Â¬ fied, wherein the first firmware (110) or the third firmware are not uniquely identified. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the second firmware (110) is uniquely associated with the processor circuitry (101), wherein the first firmware (110) is not uniquely associ Â¬ ated with the processor circuitry (101) . 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising : - encrypting the first firmware (110) or a third firm Â¬ ware using a cryptographic key uniquely associated with the processor circuitry (101) to generate the second firmware (110) . 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising : - generating control data based on the first firmware (110) or a third firmware, and 

 - embedding the control data in the first firmware (110) or the third firmware to generate the second firmware (110) . 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the control data is signed using a private cryp Â¬ tographic key which may be uniquely associated with the pro Â¬ cessor circuitry (101), wherein the control data is verifiable using a public cryptographic key. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising: - embedding the public cryptographic key in the first firmware (110) or the third firmware to generate the second firmware (110) . 8. The method of any one of claims 5 - 7, wherein the control data is uniquely associated with the processor circuitry (101). 9. The method of any one of claims 5 - 8, wherein the control data comprises a checksum of the first firmware (110) or the third firmware. 10. The method of any one of claims 5 - 9, wherein the control data comprises a cryptographic key for encryption of application executables to be executed in a runtime environment provided by the second firmware (110) and/or for encryption of application data associated with the application executables. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said disabling of the further repetition comprises at least one of deleting the first firmware (110) from the non-volatile memory (102), hardware-encoding completed execution of the first firmware (110) into the processor cir Â¬ cuitry (101), and disconnecting an external input signal as Â¬ sociated with said executing of the first firmware (110) . 12. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second firmware (110) is generated by adapt Â¬ ing a partition of the first firmware (110) or a partition of a third firmware comprising a runtime environment for execut Â¬ ing application executables, and optionally by not adapting partitions of the first firmware (110) or partitions of the third firmware comprising a bootloader and a kernel. 13. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising : wherein the second firmware (110) is generated in re Â¬ sponse to loading a kernel of the first firmware (110) . 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first firmware provides limited functionali Â¬ ty to the processor circuitry if compared to the second firm Â¬ ware . 15. A processor circuitry (101) configured to: - load, from a non-volatile memory (102), a first firm Â¬ ware (110) and executing the first firmware (110), - based on said executing of the first firmware (110) : generate a second firmware (110) and writing the second firm Â¬ ware (110) to the non-volatile memory (102), and - in response to said generating of the second firmware (110) : disable further repetitions of said executing of the first firmware (110) .
